title: ficus caulocarpa
intro: Ficus caulocarpa, also known as the 'umbrella tree', is a species of fig tree native to tropical Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Ficus caulocarpa include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions, among others. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other medicinal plants.
